SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
ByteInputStream
Input stream with support for little/big-endian multi-byte reads and position tracking.
ByteOutputStream
Output stream with support for little/big-endian multi-byte writes and position tracking.
CoffBytes
A COFF section containing raw byte data.
CoffFile
Represents a COFF (Common Object File Format) file.
CoffFile.Builder
Builder for constructing COFF files programmatically.
CoffFile.Parser
Parser for reading COFF files from an input stream.
CoffHeader
COFF file header containing machine type, timestamp, and characteristics.
CoffMachine
COFF machine identifiers.
CoffRelocationTable
COFF relocation table containing relocation entries for a section.
CoffRelocationType
Common contract for COFF relocation type enumerations.
CoffRelocationType_amd64
AMD64 COFF relocation types.
CoffRelocationType_i386
i386 COFF relocation types.
CoffSection
Base class for COFF sections.
CoffSectionFlags
Mutable builder-style wrapper around COFF section flags.
CoffSectionTable
COFF section table containing all sections in the file.
CoffStorageClass
COFF symbol storage classes.
CoffStringTable
COFF string table for storing symbol names that don't fit in the symbol table entry.
CoffSymbolTable
COFF symbol table containing symbol entries.
CoffUninitialized
A COFF section that reserves virtual space without storing raw bytes.
ElfAbiFlags_Mips
MIPS ABI flags section containing architecture-specific ABI information.
ElfClass
ELF class indicating the bit width of the file.
ElfData
ELF data encoding indicating the byte order (endianness) of the file.
ElfFile
Represents an ELF (Executable and Linkable Format) file.
ElfFile.Builder
Builder for constructing ELF files programmatically.
ElfFile.Parser
Parser for reading ELF files from an input stream.
ElfGroup
ELF section group containing a set of related sections.
ElfHeader
ELF file header containing identification, machine type, entry point, and section table information.
ElfMachine
ELF target machine architecture types.
ElfNoBits
ELF section occupying no space in the file (SHT_NOBITS).
ElfNullSection
ELF null section (SHT_NULL).
ElfOsAbi
ELF OS/ABI identification indicating the target operating system and ABI.
ElfProgBits
ELF section containing raw byte data (SHT_PROGBITS).
ElfRegInfo_Mips
MIPS register usage information section.
ElfRelaTable
ELF relocation table section with explicit addends (RELA format).
ElfRelocationType
Interface for ELF relocation types.
ElfRelocationType_amd64
AMD64 (x86-64) relocation types.
ElfRelocationType_i386
Intel 386 relocation types.
ElfRelocationType_Mips
MIPS relocation types.
ElfRelTable
ELF relocation table section without explicit addends (REL format).
ElfSection
Base class for ELF sections.
ElfSectionFlags
ELF section flags indicating section attributes.
ElfSectionFlags_Mips
MIPS-specific section flags.
ElfSectionNames
Standard ELF section names.
ElfSectionTable
ELF section table containing all sections in the file.
ElfSectionType
ELF section types defining the contents and semantics of sections.
ElfStringTable
ELF string table section for storing symbol names and section names.
ElfSymbolBinding
ELF symbol binding indicating the symbol's linkage visibility and scope.
ElfSymbolTable
ELF symbol table section containing symbol entries.
ElfSymbolType
ELF symbol types indicating what kind of entity a symbol represents.
ElfSymbolVisibility
ELF symbol visibility indicating how a symbol can be accessed.
ElfType
ELF object file type.
ElfVersion
ELF version field indicating the ELF format version.
IndirectList<T>
A list interface that delegates operations to an internal list.
IndirectNavigableMap<K,V>
A navigable map interface that delegates operations to an internal map.
MeteredOutputStream
Output stream wrapper that counts the number of bytes written through it.
StreamWritable
Interface for objects that can serialize themselves to an output stream.
TestUtils
Utility methods for testing binary file serialization and deserialization.
Utils
Utility methods for binary file processing.
Writable
Interface for objects that can be written to an output stream at a specific offset.
